name: Quebec Apple Dumplings
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F.
2. Make enough pie dough to roll into 12 squares, each measuring about 6" x 6".
3. Sprinkle 1 teaspoon grated Cheddar cheese on each square.
4. Peel and core apples; place one apple on each square.
5. In the center of each apple, add 2 teaspoons (or 1 heaping teaspoon) of minced meat, 1 teaspoon rum, a small dot of butter, and 1 tablespoon sugar.
6. Fold the dough diagonally over the apples to form ears at the top.
7. Cover the ears with aluminum foil.
8. Mix brown sugar, cream, and lemon rind together.
9. Brush the dumplings with this mixture.
10. Bake for 35 to 45 minutes in the 400°F oven.
11. Remove foil, brush the ears with the cream mixture, and return the dumplings to the oven until the ears are brown.
12. Serve warm with plain or sweetened cream, flavored with rum.
Note from Anne MacLellan: Mme Benoit did not specify total amounts for cheese, sugar, minced meat, and rum. I used Meal Master's conversion to calculate the totals.
To quote Mme. Benoit: "In Quebec, these were baked in deep brown earthenware pudding dishes and served on a hot wooden board or tray surrounded by autumn leaves or boughs cut from the Christmas tree. It was traditional in our family to serve these dumplings after we had finished decorating the Christmas tree."
